# MDLGeometryType.variableTopology

**Case**

The submesh’s index buffer does not contain a uniform set of primitives.

## Declaration

```swift
case variableTopology
```

## Discussion

If a submesh has non-uniform topology, its [doc://com.apple.modelio/documentation/ModelIO/MDLSubmesh/topology] property contains an object describing how the arrangement of values in the index buffer combine to produce the submesh’s shape.

## Constants

- **MDLGeometryType.points**: Each index in the submesh refers to a vertex to be rendered as a single point.
- **MDLGeometryType.lines**: Each pair of consecutive indices in the submesh refers to two vertices to be rendered as a line segment.
- **MDLGeometryType.triangles**: Each set of three consecutive indices in the submesh refers to three vertices to be rendered as a triangle.
- **MDLGeometryType.triangleStrips**: The first three consecutive indices in the submesh refer to three vertices to be rendered as a triangle. Each subsequent index refers to another vertex that completes a triangle formed by connecting it to the previous two vertices.
- **MDLGeometryType.quads**: Each set of four consecutive indices in the submesh refers to four vertices to be rendered as a quadrilateral.
